2606.12400.title: Doc-to-Atom: Learning to Compile and Compose Memory Atoms
2606.12400.paper_summary: Long input sequences are central to document understanding and multi-step reasoning in Large Language Models, yet the quadratic cost of attention makes inference both memory-intensive and slow. Context distillation mitigates this by compressing contextual information into model parameters, and recent work such as Doc-to-LoRA amortizes context distillation into a single forward pass that generates one LoRA adapter per document. However, producing a single monolithic adapter for all queries leads to irrelevant-query interference, limited compositional recall, and poor scalability to long-document reasoning. To address these challenges, we propose Doc-to-Atom (Doc2Atom), a compositional parametric memory framework that decomposes each document into semantically typed knowledge atoms. Each atom is compiled into an independent micro-LoRA adapter and a provenance retrieval key. At inference time, a lightweight query router selects and assembles only the relevant atoms into a query-specific adapter, which is then injected into a frozen base model. The entire system is trained end-to-end through a multi-objective distillation framework. Experiments on six diverse QA benchmarks demonstrate that Doc2Atom outperforms Doc-to-LoRA baselines while reducing the memory cost of document internalization.
2606.12400.paper_summary_zh: 長輸入序列對於大型語言模型中的文檔理解和多步推理至關重要，然而注意力的二次成本使得推理既耗費內存又緩慢。上下文蒸餾通過將上下文信息壓縮到模型參數中來減輕這一問題，最近的工作如Doc-to-LoRA將上下文蒸餾攤銷到一個單一的前向傳遞中，為每個文檔生成一個LoRA適配器。然而，為所有查詢生成一個單一的整體適配器會導致無關查詢干擾、有限的組合回憶以及對長文檔推理的擴展性差。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了Doc-to-Atom (Doc2Atom)，這是一個組合參數記憶框架，將每個文檔分解為語義類型的知識原子。每個原子被編譯成獨立的微型LoRA適配器和來源檢索鍵。在推理時，一個輕量級的查詢路由器選擇並組裝僅與查詢相關的原子到一個查詢特定的適配器中，然後注入到一個凍結的基礎模型中。整個系統通過多目標蒸餾框架進行端到端訓練。在六個不同的QA基準上的實驗表明，Doc2Atom在減少文檔內部化的內存成本的同時，超越了Doc-to-LoRA基準。
2606.12400.author: Xingjian Diao et.al.
2606.12400.authors: Xingjian Diao, Wenbo Li, Yashas Malur Saidutta, Avinash Amballa, Lazar Valkov, Srinivas Chappidi
